sections.subtitle: Team Overview and League Standing
sections.text: Eintracht Frankfurt, currently sitting at 3rd place in the league with 56 points, are in fine form as they host FC St. Pauli at the Deutsche Bank Park in Frankfurt. In stark contrast, FC St. Pauli find themselves in 15th position with only 31 points, struggling to find consistency this season. The home side's impressive league position reflects their strong attacking and defensive stats, while the visitors will be desperate to turn their fortunes around. This matchup pits a top-tier contender against a side battling to climb the table. Frankfurt's high ranking makes them clear favorites on home turf.

sections.subtitle: Historical Head-to-Head Record
sections.text: Looking at the last 5 matchups between these two sides, Eintracht Frankfurt have dominated with 3 wins compared to FC St. Pauli's single victory, alongside 1 draw. Their most recent encounter on January 11, 2025, saw Frankfurt edge out a narrow 0-1 victory away from home, with goalkeeper Kevin Trapp earning the Player of the Match accolade with a Sofascore rating of 7.9. This historical advantage gives Frankfurt a psychological edge going into this game. Meanwhile, standout player Robin Koch has been consistent in past games against St. Pauli, starting in 3 of the last 5 encounters with an average Sofascore rating of 7.17. Past results suggest Frankfurt are likely to control the narrative once again.

sections.subtitle: Key Players to Watch
sections.text: For Eintracht Frankfurt, forward Hugo Ekitiké stands out as a major threat with 15 goals and 7 assists this season, boasting an average Sofascore rating of 7.31 and recent form of 2 goals and 3 assists in the last 5 matches. Defender Nathaniel Brown has also been impactful with 3 goals and 6 assists, contributing to Frankfurt's robust defense. On the other side, FC St. Pauli's forward Morgan Guilavogui leads their attack with 5 goals and a rating of 7.0, while Noah Weißhaupt is notable for drawing fouls, ranking 5th in the league with 2.4 fouls drawn per game. However, St. Pauli will miss key midfielder Jackson Irvine, who has 6 assists despite no goals this season. Frankfurt's depth and form players give them a clear attacking edge.

sections.subtitle: Team Statistics and Playing Style
sections.text: Eintracht Frankfurt have scored 63 goals this season, ranking 4th in the league, and created 105 big chances, placing them 2nd for chance creation. Their fast breaks per game lead the league at 1.91, showcasing their lethal counter-attacking ability. Defensively, they rank 5th for fewest goals conceded with 43 and have managed 9 clean sheets, ranking 6th. FC St. Pauli, however, struggle offensively, with their goal distribution showing a reliance on late goals, as 21.6% of their strikes come between the 76th and 90th minutes. Frankfurt’s superior stats across possession (49.47%), accurate passes (83.15%, ranked 5th), and tackles per game (16.81, ranked 3rd) highlight their dominance. St. Pauli will need to disrupt Frankfurt's rhythm to stand a chance.

sections.subtitle: Match Venue and Referee Impact
sections.text: The game will be played at Deutsche Bank Park in Frankfurt, a fortress for Eintracht with a notable home streak of no losses in their last 3 matches. Referee Christian Dingert will officiate, known for averaging 4.3 yellow cards per game and 0.19 red cards per game, suggesting a potentially physical encounter with bookings likely. Both teams have streaks indicating low-scoring games, with Frankfurt having less than 2.5 goals in 7 of their last 9 matches, and St. Pauli in 8 of their last 10. However, Frankfurt’s tendency for high corner counts (more than 10.5 in 8 of their last 10 games, averaging 4.94 per game, ranked 5th) could play a factor. Dingert’s stats and team streaks suggest a game with interruptions and set-piece opportunities.

sections.subtitle: Injuries and Squad Impact
sections.text: Eintracht Frankfurt will be without midfielder Mario Götze, who has contributed 3 goals and 2 assists this season, a blow to their midfield creativity. FC St. Pauli, meanwhile, face multiple absences, including forward Elias Saad (3 goals, 2 assists) and midfielder Jackson Irvine (6 assists), which significantly weakens their attack. Defender Karol Mets, ranked 3rd in the league for aerial duels won at 70.6%, is also missing, exposing St. Pauli’s backline. These absences tilt the balance further in Frankfurt’s favor, as their squad depth can better absorb the loss. St. Pauli’s missing players could struggle to contain Frankfurt’s dynamic play.
